title: Data Warehouse Overview
content: The data warehouse runs on AWS Redshift. It follows a Kimball dimensional model with one big table (OBT) per domain and standard dimension tables (dim_member, dim_tenant, dim_date, dim_plan). All tables are prefixed by domain: rwd_ for rewards, ben_ for benefits, act_ for health actions. Raw data lands in the raw schema, transformed by dbt into the analytics schema. dbt runs nightly at 2am UTC via Airflow DAG warehouse_nightly_refresh.

title: Member Eligibility Pipeline
content: Eligibility files are delivered by clients as SFTP CSV drops to the /eligibility/{tenant_id}/ S3 bucket folder daily by 6am UTC. The eligibility_ingestion Airflow DAG picks them up, validates schema, deduplicates on member_id + effective_date, and loads into the raw.eligibility table. dbt then transforms into dim_member with active/inactive/terminated status flags. Errors are quarantined to raw.eligibility_quarantine with an error_reason column. Alerts fire to the #data-alerts Slack channel for files missing by 7am.

title: Rewards Ledger Reporting Model
content: The rwd_ledger_obt (one big table) is the source of truth for all reward transactions. Key columns: member_id, tenant_id, transaction_type (earn/redeem/expire/hold/release), amount_cents, transaction_ref_id, created_at, status. The analytics.rwd_summary_daily dbt model aggregates daily totals by tenant and transaction type. For monthly invoicing, use analytics.rwd_invoice_monthly which applies the contracted per-member fee schedule. Do not query the raw ledger directly for reporting — always use the analytics schema models.

title: LENS Dashboard — Key Metrics Definitions
content: LENS is the internal analytics dashboard (Tableau-backed). Key metric definitions: Active Member — a member who completed at least one health action in the last 90 days. Reward Attach Rate — % of active members who earned at least one reward in the period. Redemption Rate — total reward dollars redeemed / total reward dollars earned in the period. Card Utilization — % of issued cards with at least one transaction in the last 30 days. These definitions are contractually aligned with client SLAs — do not change without Client Success sign-off.

title: Synthetic Tenant Data for Testing
content: For development and QA, use the synthetic tenant 'DEMO_CORP' (tenant_id: T0000). It has 10,000 synthetic members generated with Faker, with realistic eligibility, action completion, and reward transaction histories spanning 24 months. Refresh synthetic data monthly via the `make synthetic-refresh` command. Never use real member PII in dev/test environments. The synthetic dataset is stored in the dev Redshift cluster — credentials in Vault at /secret/dev/redshift.

title: CRM Data Sync — Redshift to CRM
content: Member segmentation data is synced from Redshift to the CRM platform daily at 8am UTC via a zero-copy data share. The sync includes: member_id, active status, reward balance, last_action_date, cohort_tag, and plan_type. This data powers agent lookup during support calls and marketing segmentation. The sync job is the crm_data_sync Airflow DAG. If agents report stale member data in CRM, check this DAG first — a failed run is the most common cause.

title: Invoicing Operations
content: Monthly client invoices are generated on the 3rd business day of each month. The invoice covers: per-member-per-month (PMPM) platform fee, card issuance fees ($1.50/card), and reward distribution fees (% of redemptions). Source data: analytics.rwd_invoice_monthly and analytics.ben_invoice_monthly dbt models. Invoice generation script: scripts/invoicing/generate_invoice.py — takes tenant_id and billing_month as args. Output is a PDF + CSV pair uploaded to S3 and emailed to the client finance contact on record.

title: Data Quarantine Process
content: Records that fail validation during ingestion are written to quarantine tables (raw.*_quarantine). Each quarantine record has: source_file, error_reason, raw_row, quarantined_at. The data team reviews quarantine daily. Resolution options: fix_and_reprocess (correct the source data and re-ingest), drop (discard invalid record with documentation), or escalate_to_client (request corrected file from client). Quarantine SLA: all records resolved or escalated within 2 business days. Track in the Data Quarantine Jira board.
